Event: Peggy has her hair cut into a more fashionable flip and maintains it
Explanation: Improves her professional image and confidence with no indicated harm to others.

Event: Peggy faints when she sees a man bleeding with his foot cut off
Explanation: Her reaction incapacitates her and reduces her ability to help the victim.

Event: Peggy later keeps her head together and helps a bleeding person
Explanation: Staying calm lets her assist effectively without apparent downside.

Event: Peggy repeatedly pursues aggressive, self-centered men
Explanation: Her attraction brings her emotional trouble while rewarding inconsiderate men.

Event: Peggy ultimately chooses to get together with Stan Rizzo
Explanation: She finds a supportive partner and mutual, healthy relationship.

Event: Don chooses Peggy over Paul as his lead copywriter
Explanation: Helps Don’s work quality and advances Peggy’s career though Paul loses out professionally.

Event: Peggy hates it when people imply she slept with Don to advance
Explanation: Her anger is understandable but mainly causes her stress without changing others’ behavior.

Event: Peggy works long hours, prioritizing career over relationships
Explanation: She gains career progression at the recurring cost of her relationships.

Event: Peggy accepts that she cannot be both a productive team leader and a “nice” friend to underlings
Explanation: Adopting a firmer style improves leadership with only modest relational cost.

Event: Peggy continues to attend Mass off and on despite waning faith
Explanation: Going despite doubt burdens her yet modestly supports her community.

Event: Peggy creates an ad for Popsicles inspired by Catholic iconography
Explanation: She converts background into creative work that benefits her career and the client.

Event: Peggy gives birth to Pete’s child and gives him up for adoption
Explanation: She bears physical/psychological cost while the child likely gains a more stable life.

Event: Peggy smokes marijuana for creative inspiration
Explanation: Provides some short-term inspiration with implied health/professional risks.

Event: Peggy focuses on advancing her career while wondering about the child she gave up
Explanation: Career gains coexist with ongoing emotional burden and strained family ties.

Event: Peggy befriends and emotionally supports neighbor boy Julio
Explanation: Gives him attention and care while also gaining emotional fulfillment and growth.

Event: Peggy works her way up from shy secretary to confident Copy Chief
Explanation: Her ambition boosts her career and significantly benefits the agency’s output.

Event: Peggy expresses that she feels unattractive and struggles with dating
Explanation: Her insecurity harms her self-esteem without clear benefit to anyone else.

Event: Peggy cuts off her ponytail into an aggressive business haircut
Explanation: The haircut marks and supports her transition to a serious professional.

Event: Peggy does posters for a CYO dance
Explanation: Her work helps the event and community while demonstrating her skills.

Event: Peggy accompanies Don into the men’s room when he throws up
Explanation: She supports Don in a vulnerable moment and cements her status as “one of the guys.”

Event: Peggy acts as a parental substitute for Julio, letting him watch TV and confide in her
Explanation: She provides emotional stability and gains a meaningful connection linked to her own loss.

Event: Peggy’s lipstick-test comments get her noticed and promoted to copywriter
Explanation: Using her insight advances her career and improves the firm’s creative work.

Event: Peggy chooses to stay at work on her birthday instead of being with boyfriend and family
Explanation: She advances or protects her work status while damaging personal relationships.

Event: Peggy turns down Don’s initial demand to join SCDP until he asks properly
Explanation: She asserts her value and gains leverage while pushing Don toward respectful treatment.

Event: Peggy quits SCDP when taken for granted and accepts CGC’s better offer
Explanation: Her move greatly benefits her and CGC but harms SCDP by removing key talent.

Event: Peggy walks into McCann Erickson “with the aura of giving no fucks”
Explanation: Her confident entrance strengthens her position and earns respect.

Event: Peggy’s lack of love life turns her paranoid and more jerkish in Season 7
Explanation: Her behavior harms her well-being and strains others without any clear gain.

Event: Peggy behaves as an “Ungrateful Bastard,” antagonizing Don on his return
Explanation: Her antagonism damages their dynamic and gives her no tangible advantage.

Event: Peggy’s relationship with Don is later mended and she forgives him
Explanation: Reconciliation improves her support network and repairs a key bond.

Event: Peggy calls out Stan for slacking off and argues with him
Explanation: Challenging Stan improves work output and evolves into a stronger, honest relationship.

Event: Peggy gets together romantically with Stan
Explanation: Both gain mutual emotional support and a partner who respects her career.
